qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated remote code execution via MIME-type bypass in elFinder connector
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding exploits a MIME-type validation bypass in elFinder's mkfile/put/rename file upload chain. The advisory describes command injection in the resize command's bg parameter via ImageMagick CLI shell command construction (CWE-78). Different vulnerable functions, different root cause, different vulnerability class.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes command injection in elFinder's resize command where the bg parameter is interpolated into ImageMagick CLI shell commands without escaping (CWE-78). The unauthenticated_rce finding exploits a MIME-type bypass in elFinder's file upload/rename chain — same package but different vulnerable functions and root cause. No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause.
evidence: Advisory root cause: bg parameter injection into ImageMagick CLI shell commands in the resize handler
unauthenticated_rce root cause: MIME-type validation bypass in mkfile/put/rename file upload chain
